name: El Balcon de Alicante — Circular desde Ibi
name_original: El Balcón de Alicante — Circular desde Ibi
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Ibi, located in Alicante, Spain, traverse a diverse and challenging natural landscape. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park and the Parque Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja, which feature peaks exceeding 1,000 meters. Cyclists encounter varied environments, from pine forests and Mediterranean woodlands to dramatic rock formations and gorges, often on well-paved roads with significant elevation gains. This area offers demanding routes suitable for experienced road cyclists seeking challenging climbs and descents. Best road cycling routes around Ibi The most popular road cycling route is El Balcon de Alicante — Circular desde Ibi, a difficult 31.3 miles (50 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete, featuring over 1,100 meters of climbing. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Port de Benifallim (1015 m) – Carrasqueta Pass loop from Ibi, a difficult 44.4 miles (71 km) path. This route navigates through mountainous terrain and includes significant ascents over passes. Local road cyclists also love the Agost to Maigmó Road – Castle and Hermitage of San Pedro loop from Ibi, a 51.1 miles (82 km) trail leading through varied landscapes and past historical points of interest, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Road cycling around Ibi is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and dramatic rock formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to very challenging routes. The routes in Ibi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ibi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Ibi?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Ibi, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 3 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 18 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Ibi?
A: The road cycling routes around Ibi are characterized by diverse and challenging mountainous terrain. You'll encounter pine forests, Mediterranean woodlands, dramatic rock formations, and gorges, especially within the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park and the Parque Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja. Expect significant elevation gains and demanding climbs on generally well-paved roads.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the Ibi area?
A: While many routes in Ibi are challenging, the mention of the &lt;em&gt;Via Verda d'Alcoi&lt;/em&gt; near Ibi suggests the availability of greenways. These repurposed old railway lines typically offer flatter and more accessible routes, which can be suitable for families or cyclists looking for a less strenuous ride. It's always recommended to check the specific route details for elevation and surface conditions.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic viewpoints and pass by interesting landmarks. You could encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2874564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante Balcony&lt;/a&gt;, or ride through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1090692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agost to Maigmó Road&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2604644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3994612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Menejador&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent features. For a cultural stop, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1023814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Font Roja&lt;/a&gt; is located at approximately 1,000 meters and can be reached via demanding routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Ibi?
A: The road cycling routes in Ibi are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning natural landscapes of the Sierra de Mariola and Font Roja parks, and the well-paved roads that make for an engaging riding experience.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Ibi?
A: Yes, there are several popular circular routes starting from Ibi. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891843216/el-balcon-de-alicante-circular-desde-ibi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Balcon de Alicante — Circular desde Ibi&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 50 km trail with over 1,100 meters of climbing. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14703198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Benifallim (1015 m) – Carrasqueta Pass loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 71 km path navigating mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Ibi?
A: Given the region's Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Ibi, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially for challenging climbs, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging climbs in the Ibi area?
A: Ibi is known for its challenging climbs. The Sierra de Mariola Natural Park features peaks exceeding 1,000 meters, including Montcabrer at 1,390 meters, and the Parque Natural del Carrascal de la Font Roja has El Menejador at 1,356 meters. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14703198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Benifallim (1015 m) – Carrasqueta Pass loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt; include significant ascents over mountain passes, making them suitable for experienced cyclists seeking a demanding experience.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through gorges or dramatic natural features?
A: Absolutely. The natural parks surrounding Ibi, such as Sierra de Mariola, are known for their dramatic rock formations and gorges. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3081175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2727558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de los Molinos&lt;/a&gt;, which offer stunning natural scenery along or near cycling paths.

Q: Are there any longer road cycling routes for endurance training?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33974466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Busot – Agost to Maigmó Road loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 103 km with significant elevation gain, providing an excellent option for endurance training.

Q: What are some cultural attractions in Ibi that can complement a cycling trip?
A: After your rides, you can explore Ibi's cultural side. The town is known as the 'city of toys' and the 'cradle of ice cream.' You can visit local museums such as the Museo del Juguete (Toy Museum) or the Museo del Videojuego Arcade Vintage. The historical center, with its churches, squares, and hermitages, also offers a glimpse into the town's heritage.
tips: Honoring its name, from the balcony you will have the best views of the city of Alicante and the beautiful beaches of the Costa Blanca. A unique and ideal place, although getting there has its price, maximum slopes of between 18-20%, but I assure you that the views will more than reward that physical effort!

With its top at 1250 meters above sea level, you will have a challenge with amazing views! Getting there requires great physical effort, especially if you link it with the climb to the Balcón de Alicante, although it is worth rolling through this beautiful natural setting.
